steps.title: Spot the key limitation
steps.content: The phrase “no guarantee” signals a caveat to the success described in the previous sentences. The transition must acknowledge that success while introducing this important limitation.

steps.title: Select the concessive transition
steps.content:
“Granted,” is the word that concedes the truth of the previous statement—success with secondary objects—while introducing the caveat that these results don't guarantee success with primary objects.

The other words either suggest the results do apply to primary objects (Accordingly, Likewise) or add supporting evidence rather than introducing a caveat (Moreover).

B is correct.
